headline: Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (BUD) Stock Forecast and Price Target 2025
summary: This article provides a stock forecast and price target for Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV (BUD) for 2025. Based on analyst ratings, BUD has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $71.00, suggesting a potential upside of 16.57%. The article also details pros and cons for investors, analyst rating history, and comparisons to competitors.
label: positive

headline: Ford most at risk from China exposure among 250 American companies, consulting firm says
summary: A consulting firm, Strategy Risks, has identified Ford Motor Co. as the American company most exposed to risks from China among 250 of the largest listed U.S. companies. Ford scored 69 out of 100 on their exposure index, surpassing Apple and Tesla, due to factors like revenue in China, partnerships, and supply chain risks. Ford declined to comment on the rankings, citing a lack of detailed methodology.
label: neutral

headline: Annual Report 2024
summary: Diageo's 2024 Annual Report highlights its position as a global leader in Total Beverage Alcohol (TBA) and its ambition to be a top-performing consumer products company. The report details financial and non-financial performance, strategic priorities for growth, and sustainability efforts under its "Spirit of Progress" plan. Key executives discuss challenges from the past year and the attractive long-term growth prospects of the TBA industry, particularly in spirits and premium segments.
label: negative

headline: Dividend Growers: The Importance of Yield-on-Cost
summary: This article highlights the importance of "yield-on-cost" for dividend investors, a metric that calculates annual dividend income against the original purchase price, offering a clearer view of long-term income growth than current yield alone. It illustrates this concept with examples like the S&P 500 versus bond indexes and Warren Buffett's Coca-Cola investment, emphasizing that focusing on quality companies with a history of dividend growth is crucial for sustainable income. The piece argues that while a high current yield might seem attractive, it can often indicate higher risk, whereas steady dividend growth from stable companies can lead to significantly higher income over time.
label: positive

headline: Keurig Dr Pepper to Buy Energy-Drink Maker Ghost for Over $1 Billion
summary: Keurig Dr Pepper is set to acquire energy-drink maker Ghost for over $1 billion. This marks Keurig Dr Pepper's largest deal since its 2018 acquisition of Dr Pepper Snapple Group. The deal was confirmed on Thursday morning following an earlier report by The Wall Street Journal.
label: positive

headline: Jimmy Carter: 1924-2024
summary: The Coca-Cola Company eulogizes former U.S. President Jimmy Carter, who died December 29, 2024, at age 100, highlighting his enduring legacy of human rights advocacy and community service. The Coca-Cola Foundation will contribute $250,000 each to The Carter Center and Habitat for Humanity in his honor. The article details Carter's life, political career, Nobel Peace Prize, and long-standing relationship with The Coca-Cola Company.
label: positive

headline: The Coca-Cola Company expands longtime PGA TOUR relationship with additional category rights
summary: The Coca-Cola Company has expanded its partnership with the PGA TOUR, adding Official Sports Drink, Isotonic Drink, and Water categories to its existing designation as Official Soft Drink. This expansion will feature brands like Powerade, BODYARMOR, and smartwater, and includes various hydration and celebration programs for fans and players. The partnership, extended through 2027, also involves supporting PGA TOUR athletes and continued charitable contributions to the Atlanta community through the TOUR Championship.
label: negative

headline: Coca-Cola to “Celebrate Everyday Greatness” for Paris 2024 as it announces global athlete team for Olympic and Paralympic Games
summary: Coca-Cola has announced "Celebrate Everyday Greatness" as its theme for the Paris 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Games and unveiled its global roster of Team Coca-Cola athletes. The campaign aims to champion everyday moments that lead to greatness, featuring diverse athletes like Athing Mu and Kadeena Cox. As a Worldwide Partner, Coca-Cola will refresh athletes, officials, and spectators and continue its long-standing association with the Olympic Movement.
label: negative

headline: The Coca-Cola Company and Bacardi Limited Announce Plans to Debut BACARDÍ & Coca-Cola Ready-To-Drink Cocktail
summary: The Coca-Cola Company and Bacardi Limited have announced a partnership to launch a ready-to-drink (RTD) BACARDÍ rum and Coca-Cola pre-mixed cocktail. This RTD product will initially debut in select European markets and Mexico in 2025, expanding the beverage portfolios of both companies into the growing alcoholic RTD market. The product will highlight the historical connection between the two brands and include responsibility symbols for legal drinking age consumers.
label: negative

headline: Coca-Cola HBC ranked again as the world's most sustainable beverage company
summary: Coca-Cola HBC has been ranked as the world's most sustainable beverage company for the seventh time by the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices (DJSI) and featured among the top three performers for the 13th consecutive year. The company also achieved a double-A rating from the CDP for transparency on climate and water. These rankings solidify Coca-Cola HBC's position in the top 1% of 9,400 companies globally for sustainability.
label: negative

headline: Coca-Cola manufacturing and distribution facility open on Billings West End
summary: Coca-Cola Bottling Company High Country has opened a new 182,600-square-foot manufacturing and distribution facility in Billings, Montana, two years after breaking ground. This nearly $100 million investment is their largest to date, creating 50 new jobs and retaining 60 existing positions. The facility, which is family-owned, will bottle and can thousands of products daily and will host a public tour.
label: negative

headline: Netflix Executive Bela Bajaria Elected to Board of Directors of The Coca-Cola Company
summary: Bela Bajaria, Chief Content Officer for Netflix Inc., has been elected to The Coca-Cola Company's board of directors, effective immediately, bringing the board to 12 directors. Bajaria oversees all television and film for Netflix and manages a content budget of about $17 billion annually, bringing extensive global business and media experience to Coca-Cola. The company also declared a regular quarterly dividend of 48.5 cents per common share, payable Dec. 16.
label: positive

headline: PepsiCo to replace Coca-Cola at Subway’s US locations
summary: PepsiCo Inc. has secured a 10-year agreement to become the exclusive beverage provider for all Subway sandwich shops in the US, replacing Coca-Cola Co. Coca-Cola brands will start to be phased out in January 2025. This deal also extends PepsiCo's Frito-Lay agreement to supply snacks to Subway through 2030, reinforcing PepsiCo's presence in one of the nation's largest restaurant chains.
label: negative

headline: Coke, Pepsi, and Campbell Soup Yank Funding for Deadly Animal Experiments
summary: Following PETA's intervention, five major food and beverage companies—PepsiCo. Inc., The Coca-Cola Company, Campbell Soup Company, Fresh Innovations, and Sensient Technologies—have ceased funding for animal experimentation. The companies were informed that their contributions were supporting cruel experiments at the University of Wisconsin–Madison’s Food Research Institute. The Coca-Cola Company has completely withdrawn sponsorship, while the others have imposed restrictions to prevent their money from funding animal tests.
label: neutral

headline: From Tide Pods to Coach bags, how Fortune 500 companies use museums of their hits and misses to drive success
summary: Fortune 500 companies are increasingly utilizing in-house museums and archives to drive success by learning from past product hits and misses. These collections, like P&G's "Wall of Failures" and Coach's handbag archive, serve practical purposes beyond nostalgia, guiding product development, C-suite decision-making, and culture-building among employees. The article highlights how brands like Delta, Walmart, Lego, Reckitt, and Coca-Cola leverage their histories to inspire innovation, foster a sense of purpose, and navigate challenges.
label: positive
